assumptions.assumption: IdP must be reachable for Identity Provider (IdP).

assumptions.verification_method: Test IdP failover and offline access modes
assumptions.example_scenario: Identity Provider (IdP) authenticates via SSO

assumptions.assumption: SSO session lifetime is appropriate for risk level for Identity Provider (IdP).
assumptions.risk: Overly long sessions bypass per-application auth
assumptions.verification_method: Configure session duration based on application risk classification
assumptions.example_scenario: Identity Provider (IdP) authenticates via SSO

assumptions.assumption: SSO covers all applications for Identity Provider (IdP).

assumptions.example_scenario: Identity Provider (IdP) authenticates via SSO

assumptions.example_scenario: Identity Provider (IdP) authenticates via SSO

assumptions.assumption: IdP trust configuration is correct for Identity Provider (IdP).
assumptions.risk: Misconfigured IdP trust allows unauthorized access

assumptions.example_scenario: Identity Provider (IdP) authenticates via SSO

assumptions.assumption: SAML/OIDC assertions are signed and validated for Identity Provider (IdP).

assumptions.verification_method: Enforce assertion signing and validate signatures on every request
assumptions.example_scenario: Identity Provider (IdP) authenticates via SSO

assumptions.assumption: Federation partner metadata is up to date for Identity Provider (IdP).

assumptions.assumption: Single logout propagates to all applications for Identity Provider (IdP).

assumptions.example_scenario: Identity Provider (IdP) authenticates via SSO

assumptions.assumption: JIT provisioning creates correct accounts for Identity Provider (IdP).

assumptions.example_scenario: Identity Provider (IdP) authenticates via SSO

assumptions.assumption: Segment is isolated from unauthorized networks for Production VPC via Internet Gateway.
assumptions.risk: Cross-segment traffic bypasses security controls
assumptions.verification_method: Test network ACLs and security groups with active scanning
assumptions.example_scenario: Traffic from Production VPC traverses Internet Gateway

assumptions.assumption: Route tables direct traffic to correct destinations for Production VPC via Internet Gateway.
assumptions.risk: Misrouted traffic leaves the network boundary
assumptions.verification_method: Audit route tables for unintended default routes
assumptions.example_scenario: Traffic from Production VPC traverses Internet Gateway

assumptions.assumption: Network ACLs filter traffic in both directions for Production VPC via Internet Gateway.
assumptions.risk: Stateless ACLs only filtering inbound creates outbound bypass
assumptions.verification_method: Verify NACL rules cover both inbound and outbound directions
assumptions.example_scenario: Traffic from Production VPC traverses Internet Gateway

assumptions.assumption: Security groups are scoped to specific CIDRs, not 0.0.0.0/0 for Production VPC via Internet Gateway.
assumptions.risk: Overly permissive security groups expose services to internet
assumptions.verification_method: Review all security groups with 0.0.0.0/0 inbound rules
assumptions.example_scenario: Traffic from Production VPC traverses Internet Gateway

assumptions.assumption: VPC peering does not extend beyond approved accounts for Production VPC via Internet Gateway.
assumptions.risk: Peering across accounts creates unauthorized network paths
assumptions.verification_method: Audit VPC peering connections quarterly
assumptions.example_scenario: Traffic from Production VPC traverses Internet Gateway

assumptions.assumption: VPC endpoints are configured for private access for Production VPC via Internet Gateway.
assumptions.risk: Traffic to AWS services traverses internet instead of AWS network
assumptions.verification_method: Verify VPC endpoint policies and route tables
assumptions.example_scenario: Traffic from Production VPC traverses Internet Gateway

assumptions.assumption: No subnet is unintentionally public for Production VPC via Internet Gateway.
assumptions.risk: Subnet with IGW route + public IP is directly internet accessible
assumptions.verification_method: Scan for subnets with 0.0.0.0/0 route to IGW
assumptions.example_scenario: Traffic from Production VPC traverses Internet Gateway

assumptions.assumption: Network flow logs are enabled for critical segments for Production VPC via Internet Gateway.
assumptions.risk: Without flow logs, malicious traffic is invisible
assumptions.verification_method: Enable VPC flow logs for all production subnets
assumptions.example_scenario: Traffic from Production VPC traverses Internet Gateway

assumptions.example_scenario: Data written to RDS database instance is encrypted via KMS key

assumptions.assumption: Key backup exists and is recoverable for RDS database instance backed by KMS key.
assumptions.risk: Key loss equals permanent data loss
assumptions.verification_method: Test key backup restoration procedure annually
assumptions.example_scenario: Data written to RDS database instance is encrypted via KMS key

assumptions.example_scenario: Data written to RDS database instance is encrypted via KMS key

assumptions.example_scenario: Data written to RDS database instance is encrypted via KMS key

assumptions.assumption: Key management system is available for RDS database instance backed by KMS key.
assumptions.risk: KMS outage blocks all encrypted data access
assumptions.verification_method: Test KMS failover and understand blast radius of KMS region outage
assumptions.example_scenario: Data written to RDS database instance is encrypted via KMS key

assumptions.assumption: Key compromise incident response exists for RDS database instance backed by KMS key.
assumptions.risk: No process for key revocation leaves data exposed
assumptions.verification_method: Document and test key compromise response procedure
assumptions.example_scenario: Data written to RDS database instance is encrypted via KMS key

assumptions.assumption: Encryption mode is secure (GCM, not ECB) for RDS database instance backed by KMS key.
assumptions.risk: ECB mode leaks data patterns in encryption output
assumptions.verification_method: Verify encryption algorithm uses authenticated encryption mode
assumptions.example_scenario: Data written to RDS database instance is encrypted via KMS key

assumptions.assumption: HSM is not physically compromised for RDS database instance backed by KMS key.
assumptions.risk: HSM tampering exposes all keys protected by it
assumptions.verification_method: Verify HSM is in tamper-resistant enclosure with audit trail
assumptions.example_scenario: Data written to RDS database instance is encrypted via KMS key

assumptions.risk: Excessive permissions violate least privilege
assumptions.verification_method: Review IAM Access Advisor metrics and rightsize permissions
assumptions.example_scenario: K requests access to resource

assumptions.assumption: Permission boundaries are enforced for K scoped by Key users.
assumptions.risk: IAM boundary does not restrict permission escalation paths
assumptions.verification_method: Test that permission boundary stops privilege escalation
assumptions.example_scenario: K requests access to resource

assumptions.assumption: Permission requests include business justification for K scoped by Key users.
assumptions.risk: Unchallenged permission requests accumulate unchecked
assumptions.verification_method: Require ticket number and approval for every permission grant
assumptions.example_scenario: K requests access to resource

assumptions.assumption: Temporary permissions have expiration for K scoped by Key users.
assumptions.risk: Time-unlimited elevated permissions become standing privileges
assumptions.verification_method: Implement temporary credential mechanisms (STS, JIT)
assumptions.example_scenario: K requests access to resource

assumptions.assumption: Permission creep is detected and remediated for K scoped by Key users.
assumptions.risk: Users accumulate permissions over time without review
assumptions.verification_method: Run access review automation comparing current vs required permissions
assumptions.example_scenario: K requests access to resource

assumptions.assumption: Privilege elevation requires separate auth for K scoped by Key users.
assumptions.risk: Always-elevated sessions increase blast radius of compromise
assumptions.verification_method: Implement just-in-time elevation with audit trail
assumptions.example_scenario: K requests access to resource

assumptions.assumption: Break-glass emergency access is monitored for K scoped by Key users.
assumptions.risk: Emergency access procedures can be abused for unauthorized access
assumptions.verification_method: Audit all break-glass usage within 24 hours and review monthly
assumptions.example_scenario: K requests access to resource

assumptions.assumption: Group/permission inheritance does not create unintended access for K scoped by Key users.

assumptions.example_scenario: K requests access to resource

assumptions.assumption: Cross-account access is scoped and reviewed for K scoped by Key users.

assumptions.example_scenario: K requests access to resource

assumptions.assumption: Default deny is enforced for all access requests for K scoped by Key users.

assumptions.assumption: All critical systems are sending logs for Production application logs delivered via Log agent + pipeline.
assumptions.risk: Unlogged systems are invisible during incident investigation
assumptions.verification_method: Audit log source coverage against asset inventory quarterly
assumptions.example_scenario: Security event occurs in Production application logs

assumptions.assumption: Log storage has sufficient capacity for retention period for Production application logs delivered via Log agent + pipeline.
assumptions.risk: Logs rotated before compliance period due to capacity limits
assumptions.verification_method: Monitor log storage utilization and auto-scale or alert
assumptions.example_scenario: Security event occurs in Production application logs

assumptions.assumption: Logs are tamper-proof (immutable) for Production application logs delivered via Log agent + pipeline.
assumptions.risk: Attacker covering tracks can modify logs to hide activity
assumptions.verification_method: Implement immutable log storage with separate admin access controls
assumptions.example_scenario: Security event occurs in Production application logs

assumptions.assumption: Alerts are configured for key detection events for Production application logs delivered via Log agent + pipeline.
assumptions.risk: Security events without alerts are invisible
assumptions.verification_method: Review alert rule coverage against ATT&CK framework
assumptions.example_scenario: Security event occurs in Production application logs

assumptions.assumption: Alerts are triaged within defined SLA for Production application logs delivered via Log agent + pipeline.
assumptions.risk: Un-triaged alerts accumulate and critical events are missed
assumptions.verification_method: Monitor mean time to triage (MTTT) and alert backlog
assumptions.example_scenario: Security event occurs in Production application logs

assumptions.assumption: Log retention meets compliance requirements for Production application logs delivered via Log agent + pipeline.
assumptions.risk: Logs deleted before compliance period create audit failures
assumptions.verification_method: Verify log retention settings against compliance calendar
assumptions.example_scenario: Security event occurs in Production application logs

assumptions.assumption: Monitoring covers all environments (prod, staging, dev) for Production application logs delivered via Log agent + pipeline.
assumptions.risk: Security events in non-prod environments go undetected
assumptions.verification_method: Extend monitoring agent deployment to all environments
assumptions.example_scenario: Security event occurs in Production application logs

assumptions.assumption: Log correlation across sources is operational for Production application logs delivered via Log agent + pipeline.
assumptions.risk: Siloed logs miss multi-stage attack sequences
assumptions.verification_method: Test cross-source correlation scenarios quarterly
assumptions.example_scenario: Security event occurs in Production application logs

assumptions.assumption: Anomaly detection has baseline of normal behavior for Production application logs delivered via Log agent + pipeline.
assumptions.risk: Without baseline, anomaly detection generates excessive noise
assumptions.verification_method: Establish and tune behavioral baselines per system
assumptions.example_scenario: Security event occurs in Production application logs

assumptions.assumption: Alerts integrate with response workflow (PagerDuty, Slack, etc.) for Production application logs delivered via Log agent + pipeline.
assumptions.risk: Alerts that nobody sees might as well not exist
assumptions.verification_method: Test alert notification delivery path monthly
assumptions.example_scenario: Security event occurs in Production application logs

assumptions.assumption: Running containers are scanned for vulnerabilities for Container base image via Container registry.

assumptions.assumption: TLS certificates are valid and not expired for Public-facing web application using TLS certificate.
assumptions.risk: Expired certificate causes service outage or insecure fallback
assumptions.verification_method: Monitor certificate expiry and auto-renew with 30-day notice
assumptions.example_scenario: Client connects to Public-facing web application

assumptions.assumption: TLS is enforced on ALL connections, not optional for Public-facing web application using TLS certificate.
assumptions.risk: Downgrade attacks can force plaintext fallback
assumptions.verification_method: Enforce HSTS and reject non-TLS connections at load balancer
assumptions.example_scenario: Client connects to Public-facing web application

assumptions.assumption: Minimum TLS version 1.2 is enforced for Public-facing web application using TLS certificate.
assumptions.risk: TLS 1.0/1.1 are vulnerable to protocol downgrade attacks
assumptions.verification_method: Configure server to reject TLS versions below 1.2
assumptions.example_scenario: Client connects to Public-facing web application

assumptions.assumption: Cipher suites are strong (no RC4, 3DES, CBC) for Public-facing web application using TLS certificate.
assumptions.risk: Weak ciphers enable decryption by sophisticated attackers
assumptions.verification_method: Audit cipher suite configuration against Mozilla SSL guidelines
assumptions.example_scenario: Client connects to Public-facing web application

assumptions.assumption: mTLS is used for service-to-service communication for Public-facing web application using TLS certificate.
assumptions.risk: Without mTLS, server cannot verify client identity
assumptions.verification_method: Implement mTLS via service mesh or certificate-based auth
assumptions.example_scenario: Client connects to Public-facing web application

assumptions.assumption: Certificate revocation is checked for Public-facing web application using TLS certificate.
assumptions.risk: Compromised certificate continues to be trusted
assumptions.verification_method: Enable OCSP stapling or CRL checking on all endpoints
assumptions.example_scenario: Client connects to Public-facing web application

assumptions.assumption: Private keys are protected (not in code, not world-readable) for Public-facing web application using TLS certificate.
assumptions.risk: Exposed private key allows traffic decryption and impersonation
assumptions.verification_method: Store private keys in HSM or secrets manager with access audit
assumptions.example_scenario: Client connects to Public-facing web application

assumptions.assumption: Certificate chain is complete and trusted by clients for Public-facing web application using TLS certificate.
assumptions.risk: Incomplete cert chain causes browser/client trust warnings
assumptions.verification_method: Verify full certificate chain is served including intermediates
assumptions.example_scenario: Client connects to Public-facing web application

assumptions.assumption: SNI routing is correctly configured for multi-domain for Public-facing web application using TLS certificate.
assumptions.risk: Incorrect SNI routing sends traffic to wrong backend
assumptions.verification_method: Test SNI routing for all hostnames on shared IP
assumptions.example_scenario: Client connects to Public-facing web application

assumptions.assumption: OCSP responder is reachable for real-time validation for Public-facing web application using TLS certificate.
assumptions.risk: Unreachable OCSP responder fails open (accepts all) or closed
assumptions.verification_method: Configure OCSP stapling to avoid OCSP verification failures
assumptions.example_scenario: Client connects to Public-facing web application

assumptions.assumption: All changes follow the defined change process for Production deployment requiring Change approval.
assumptions.risk: Unauthorized changes create undocumented configuration drift
assumptions.verification_method: Audit change tickets against actual infrastructure changes
assumptions.example_scenario: Team performs Production deployment

assumptions.assumption: Emergency changes are reviewed post-hoc for Production deployment requiring Change approval.
assumptions.risk: Emergency changes bypassing review accumulate risk
assumptions.verification_method: Conduct post-emergency change review within 48 hours
assumptions.example_scenario: Team performs Production deployment

assumptions.assumption: Change documentation accurately describes what changed for Production deployment requiring Change approval.
assumptions.risk: Inaccurate documentation misleads incident responders
assumptions.verification_method: Verify change documentation completeness within 24 hours of change
assumptions.example_scenario: Team performs Production deployment

assumptions.assumption: Changes are tested in non-production before deployment for Production deployment requiring Change approval.
assumptions.risk: Untested changes in production cause preventable incidents
assumptions.verification_method: Enforce change promotion from dev → staging → prod pipeline
assumptions.example_scenario: Team performs Production deployment

assumptions.assumption: Rollback procedure exists and is tested for Production deployment requiring Change approval.
assumptions.risk: Failed change without rollback leads to extended outage
assumptions.verification_method: Include and test rollback plan in every change ticket
assumptions.example_scenario: Team performs Production deployment

assumptions.assumption: Changes occur within approved change windows for Production deployment requiring Change approval.
assumptions.risk: Out-of-window changes bypass normal review
assumptions.verification_method: Alert on infrastructure changes outside approved windows
assumptions.example_scenario: Team performs Production deployment

assumptions.assumption: Configuration drift is detected after change for Production deployment requiring Change approval.
assumptions.risk: Manual changes outside IaC create unreproducible environment
assumptions.verification_method: Run drift detection after every change and remediate automatically
assumptions.example_scenario: Team performs Production deployment

assumptions.assumption: Change approval comes from authorized approver for Production deployment requiring Change approval.
assumptions.risk: Rubber-stamped approvals defeat change management purpose
assumptions.verification_method: Rotate change approver assignments and audit approval patterns
assumptions.example_scenario: Team performs Production deployment

assumptions.assumption: Change notifications reach all affected stakeholders for Production deployment requiring Change approval.
assumptions.risk: Undisclosed changes surprise downstream teams
assumptions.verification_method: Implement automatic notification distribution based on change impact
assumptions.example_scenario: Team performs Production deployment

assumptions.assumption: Changes do not conflict with other planned changes for Production deployment requiring Change approval.
assumptions.risk: Conflicting changes cause cascading failures
assumptions.verification_method: Require change scheduling coordination for dependent systems
assumptions.example_scenario: Team performs Production deployment

assumptions.assumption: IAM policies allow only required actions for AWS IAM user via IAM policy.
assumptions.risk: Overly permissive IAM policies enable privilege escalation
assumptions.verification_method: Use IAM Access Analyzer to identify unused permissions
assumptions.example_scenario: Cloud resource access via AWS IAM user

assumptions.assumption: IAM roles are used instead of long-lived user credentials for AWS IAM user via IAM policy.
assumptions.risk: Long-lived access keys create rotation and exposure risk
assumptions.verification_method: Migrate workloads to instance profiles/IRSA with temporary credentials
assumptions.example_scenario: Cloud resource access via AWS IAM user

assumptions.assumption: IAM role trust policies are restrictive for AWS IAM user via IAM policy.
assumptions.risk: Overly broad trust policy allows unauthorized account to assume role
assumptions.verification_method: Restrict trust policy to specific accounts and external IDs
assumptions.example_scenario: Cloud resource access via AWS IAM user

assumptions.assumption: IAM access keys are rotated regularly for AWS IAM user via IAM policy.
assumptions.risk: Stale access keys increase blast radius of credential leak
assumptions.verification_method: Automate access key rotation and monitor key age
assumptions.example_scenario: Cloud resource access via AWS IAM user

assumptions.assumption: CloudTrail is enabled across all regions for AWS IAM user via IAM policy.
assumptions.risk: Activity in un-trailed regions is invisible to security team
assumptions.verification_method: Enable multi-region CloudTrail trail with organization trail
assumptions.example_scenario: Cloud resource access via AWS IAM user

assumptions.assumption: GuardDuty findings are reviewed and triaged for AWS IAM user via IAM policy.
assumptions.risk: Critical findings in GuardDuty backlog are missed
assumptions.verification_method: Integrate GuardDuty with SIEM and set up alert routing
assumptions.example_scenario: Cloud resource access via AWS IAM user

assumptions.assumption: S3 bucket policies are restrictive (no public access) for AWS IAM user via IAM policy.
assumptions.risk: Public S3 buckets leak sensitive data continuously
assumptions.verification_method: Enable S3 Block Public Access at account level
assumptions.example_scenario: Cloud resource access via AWS IAM user

assumptions.assumption: AWS Config rules monitor for compliance drift for AWS IAM user via IAM policy.
assumptions.risk: Unmonitored configuration changes create undetected policy violations
assumptions.verification_method: Implement AWS Config conformance packs for security benchmarks
assumptions.example_scenario: Cloud resource access via AWS IAM user

assumptions.assumption: SCPs enforce guardrails across all accounts for AWS IAM user via IAM policy.
assumptions.risk: Member accounts can opt out of security controls without SCP enforcement
assumptions.verification_method: Apply SCPs to prevent security control disabling
assumptions.example_scenario: Cloud resource access via AWS IAM user

assumptions.assumption: IAM Access Analyzer is enabled and findings reviewed for AWS IAM user via IAM policy.
assumptions.risk: Unintended resource exposure goes undetected without Access Analyzer

assumptions.risk: Disabled EDR agent provides no protection
assumptions.verification_method: Monitor EDR agent heartbeat and alert on check-in failures
assumptions.example_scenario: Endpoint Corporate laptop is compromised

assumptions.assumption: Anti-malware signatures are current for Corporate laptop via EDR agent.
assumptions.risk: Outdated signatures miss recent malware variants
assumptions.verification_method: Verify signature age < 24 hours across all endpoints
assumptions.example_scenario: Endpoint Corporate laptop is compromised

assumptions.assumption: OS and application patches are applied within SLA for Corporate laptop via EDR agent.
assumptions.risk: Unpatched vulnerabilities are actively exploited in the wild
assumptions.verification_method: Deploy patch management with automated enforcement
assumptions.example_scenario: Endpoint Corporate laptop is compromised

assumptions.assumption: Full disk encryption is enabled for Corporate laptop via EDR agent.
assumptions.risk: Lost/stolen device exposes all data without FDE
assumptions.verification_method: Verify FDE status via MDM or EDR reporting
assumptions.example_scenario: Endpoint Corporate laptop is compromised

assumptions.assumption: Local admin rights are restricted for Corporate laptop via EDR agent.
assumptions.risk: Users with local admin can disable security controls
assumptions.verification_method: Remove local admin rights and implement LAPS for admin passwords
assumptions.example_scenario: Endpoint Corporate laptop is compromised

assumptions.assumption: Application allowlisting/blacklisting is enforced for Corporate laptop via EDR agent.
assumptions.risk: Users can install unapproved applications with malware risk
assumptions.verification_method: Deploy application control policies via MDM/GPO
assumptions.example_scenario: Endpoint Corporate laptop is compromised

assumptions.assumption: USB device control is enforced for Corporate laptop via EDR agent.
assumptions.risk: Malware introduced via USB bypasses network controls
assumptions.verification_method: Restrict USB mass storage and enable audit logging
assumptions.example_scenario: Endpoint Corporate laptop is compromised

assumptions.risk: Unpatched vulnerabilities are actively exploited in the wild
assumptions.verification_method: Deploy patch management with automated enforcement
assumptions.example_scenario: Endpoint Mobile device is compromised

assumptions.assumption: Full disk encryption is enabled for Mobile device via MDM profile.
assumptions.risk: Lost/stolen device exposes all data without FDE
assumptions.verification_method: Verify FDE status via MDM or EDR reporting
assumptions.example_scenario: Endpoint Mobile device is compromised

assumptions.assumption: Local admin rights are restricted for Mobile device via MDM profile.
assumptions.risk: Users with local admin can disable security controls
assumptions.verification_method: Remove local admin rights and implement LAPS for admin passwords
assumptions.example_scenario: Endpoint Mobile device is compromised

assumptions.assumption: Application allowlisting/blacklisting is enforced for Mobile device via MDM profile.
assumptions.risk: Users can install unapproved applications with malware risk
assumptions.verification_method: Deploy application control policies via MDM/GPO
assumptions.example_scenario: Endpoint Mobile device is compromised

assumptions.assumption: USB device control is enforced for Mobile device via MDM profile.
assumptions.risk: Malware introduced via USB bypasses network controls
assumptions.verification_method: Restrict USB mass storage and enable audit logging
assumptions.example_scenario: Endpoint Mobile device is compromised

assumptions.assumption: Physical access is restricted to authorized personnel only for D via a.

assumptions.verification_method: Audit physical access logs and revoke departed employee badges

assumptions.risk: Tailgating bypasses badge access control completely
assumptions.verification_method: Implement mantraps or turnstiles at data center entrances
assumptions.example_scenario: Physical access attempt at D

assumptions.assumption: Visitor access is logged and escorted for D via a.
assumptions.risk: Unescorted visitor has unrestricted physical access
assumptions.verification_method: Enforce visitor check-in process and escort policy
assumptions.example_scenario: Physical access attempt at D

assumptions.assumption: Biometric system has alternate authentication method for D via a.
assumptions.risk: Biometric failure denies access or forces security-compromising bypass
assumptions.verification_method: Maintain alternative access method and emergency access protocol
assumptions.example_scenario: Physical access attempt at D

assumptions.assumption: Temperature and humidity are monitored for D via a.
assumptions.risk: Cooling failure causes hardware damage and data loss
assumptions.verification_method: Implement environmental monitoring with automated alerting
assumptions.example_scenario: Physical access attempt at D

assumptions.assumption: UPS and generator provide continuous power for D via a.

assumptions.verification_method: Test UPS battery capacity and generator under load quarterly
assumptions.example_scenario: Physical access attempt at D

assumptions.assumption: Fire suppression system is operational for D via a.
assumptions.risk: Fire destroys hardware and data permanently
assumptions.verification_method: Inspect fire suppression system and test detection annually
